name: Overijssel (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: from the gules of the historic provincial arms
from the or of the historic provincial arms

description: A horizontally tribanded field of red, yellow, and red in proportions 1:3:1, with a blue wavy band running horizontally across the centre of the yellow stripe.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 11 July 1948. The blue wave evokes the IJssel, the river that defines the province's name and its western boundary.

features.symbolism: the red and yellow are taken from the arms of the historic territory; the central blue wavy band represents the river IJssel, from which the province takes its name (Over-IJssel: the lands beyond the IJssel)
the river IJssel that gives the province its name
